content.problemStatement:
Grapes of Coderpur are very famous. Devu went to the market and saw that there were **N** people selling grapes. He didn’t like it because things were not very structured. So, he gave a task to Dhinwa to make things better. If Dhinwa successfully completes the task, Devu will be happy.


 
Devu wants to change the number of grapes in a bucket of zero or more sellers in such a way that the GCD  of all the number of grapes is divisible by K. Dhinwa can add or remove any number of grapes from each of the buckets. Adding or removing a grape will be counted as an operation. Also after the operation, none of the seller’s bucket should be empty.



Help Dhinwa in finding the minimum number of operations needed to make Devu happy.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and K. The next line contains N space-separated integers a1, a2, …, aN – the initial number of grapes in each bucket.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required so that after the operations the GCD of all bucket sizes is divisible by K (i.e., every bucket size becomes a positive multiple of K).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
First test case: change 3→2 (1 operation) and 5→4 (1 operation). Total 2.
Second test case: 10→7 (3), 16→14 (2), 18→21 (3). Total 8.

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the remainder of each bucket size when divided by K. If the remainder is 0, the bucket size is already a multiple of K. Otherwise, we need to either add or remove grapes to make the bucket size a multiple of K. We choose the operation that requires the minimum number of grapes to be added or removed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and the result.
